name: Zapatista Local Autonomous Governments (GAL) Restructuring
anti_capture_mechanisms: reactive_decentralization: dissolved centralized MAREZ and Good Government Juntas under cartel/state pressure — moved to thousands of Local Autonomous Governments (GALs)
base_community_governance: each GAL has own assembly, own governance, own relationships — no shared killable coordinator
legibility_reduction: caracoles remain as meeting places, not command structures; named targets dissolved
distributed_below_threshold: governance moved to most granular level possible
outcome: Active. Prior structure (30-year MAREZ/Juntas model) was legible enough to be pressure-targeted by cartels and state. Restructuring trades some coordination capacity for survival architecture. Ongoing tension: same decentralization that protects against capture makes unified recruitment surface harder. Youth exodus from communities is partly a result of isolation protection cost.

fifth_question.rationale: GALs are the confrontational organizational form — autonomous governance holding territory against state and cartel power, not demonstrating around it.

notes: Reactive anti-capture architecture — applied after vulnerability identified, unlike Hampton's embedded-from-start design. Live example of the legibility-for-solidarity vs. illegibility-for-survival tension. The Zapatistas are living this trade-off in real time and have not resolved it cleanly — which is what makes this case honest.
